title: GKE Pipeline using Cloud Build (CI/CD)
aim: To create a CI/CD pipeline that builds Docker images with Cloud Build, stores them in Artifact Registry, and deploys to Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) automatically.

steps.title: Initialize the Lab
steps.description: Enable APIs (GKE, Cloud Build, Artifact Registry). Create a Docker repository in Artifact Registry and a GKE cluster.

steps.title: Set Up Repositories
steps.description: Create Cloud Source Repositories for the app code and the environment config. Clone the sample app code.

steps.title: Build Container Image
steps.description: Use 'gcloud builds submit' to build a Docker image from the source code and push it to Artifact Registry.

steps.title: Configure CI Pipeline
steps.description: Create a Cloud Build trigger that runs whenever code is pushed to the repository. The build is defined in 'cloudbuild.yaml'.

steps.title: Deploy to GKE (CD)
steps.description: Create a CD pipeline that applies the Kubernetes manifests to the GKE cluster using 'kubectl apply'.

commands.purpose: Create a Docker repository in Artifact Registry
Manually trigger a build and push to Artifact Registry

viva.question: What is CI/CD?
viva.answer: Continuous Integration (CI) is the practice of automating the building and testing of code. Continuous Deployment (CD) automates the delivery of that code to a production-like environment.

viva.question: What is Cloud Build?
viva.answer: A serverless build service that executes builds on Google Cloud. It can build Docker images, binaries, and more.

viva.question: What is Artifact Registry?
viva.answer: A managed service for storing and managing build artifacts like Docker images and language packages (Maven, npm).

notes: The pipeline is often triggered by a git push to a specific branch.
